error.invalid_month_value: One or more month values are invalid. Months must be integers between 1 and 12.

data_description.hsem_batteries_enable_excess_export: Enable or disable the excess battery export feature, which automatically exports excess battery capacity to the grid when economically beneficial.
data_description.hsem_batteries_excess_export_discharge_b: Set the safety buffer percentage to maintain minimum battery reserves (0-50%). Default is 10% to ensure adequate capacity for unexpected demand.

batteries_excess_export.description: Configure excess battery export settings to automatically sell excess energy to the grid when economically beneficial.
batteries_excess_export.title: Excess Battery Export

data_description.hsem_ev_planned_load_enabled: Enable or disable planned EV charging load integration.
data_description.hsem_ev_planned_load_battery_capacity_kw: Nameplate battery capacity of the EV in kWh.
data_description.hsem_ev_planned_load_charger_power_kw: AC output power of the EV charger in kW.
data_description.hsem_ev_planned_load_charger_efficiency: Charger efficiency as a percentage (1-100). Default: 100.
data_description.hsem_ev_planned_load_charger_min_power_w: Minimum AC power (W) the EV charger needs to start charging. Below this the charger will not operate. Default: 1380 W (230 V Ã— 6 A).

ev_planned_load.description: Configure optional planned EV charging load integration. When enabled, HSEM accounts for upcoming EV charging demand per planner slot.
ev_planned_load.title: EV Planned Load Integration

data_description.hsem_ev_second_planned_load_enabled: Enable or disable planned charging load integration for the second EV.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_planned_load_battery_capa: Nameplate battery capacity of the second EV in kWh.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_planned_load_charger_powe: AC output power of the second EV charger in kW.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_planned_load_charger_effi: Second EV charger efficiency as a percentage (1-100). Default: 100.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_planned_load_charger_min_: Minimum AC power (W) the second EV charger needs to start charging. Default: 1380 W (230 V Ã— 6 A).

ev_second_planned_load.description: Configure optional planned charging load integration for the second EV. Only shown when a second EV charger is enabled.
ev_second_planned_load.title: EV 2 Planned Load Integration

data_description.hsem_ocpp_enabled: Enable the embedded OCPP 1.6 WebSocket server for direct EV charger control. LAN-only — do not expose the port to the internet.
data_description.hsem_ocpp_port: TCP port for the OCPP WebSocket server. Default: 9000. Must be above 1024.
data_description.hsem_ocpp_cpid: Expected charge-point identifier of the EV charger. Leave empty to accept any charger.
data_description.hsem_ocpp_start_window_s: Seconds of sustained surplus required before starting a charge. Prevents rapid start-stop cycling. Default: 60.
data_description.hsem_ocpp_stop_window_s: Seconds of sustained shortage required before stopping a charge. Prevents rapid stop-start cycling. Default: 180.

ocpp.description: Configure the embedded OCPP 1.6 server for LAN-only EV charger control. When enabled, HSEM sends SetChargingProfile commands directly to your EV charger based on the charging plan.
ocpp.title: OCPP Server

data_description.hsem_import_electricity_price_sensor: Select the sensor that provides import electricity prices (e.g. Energi Data Service, Nordpool, Amber Electric).
data_description.hsem_export_electricity_price_sensor: Select the sensor that provides export electricity prices.
data_description.hsem_import_electricity_price_forecast_s: Optional: select a separate sensor for import price forecasts (used by Amber Electric and similar integrations).
data_description.hsem_export_electricity_price_forecast_s: Optional: select a separate sensor for export price forecasts.
data_description.hsem_export_electricity_min_price: Set the minimum export price. Exports below this price will be blocked.
data_description.hsem_electricity_price_update_interval: How often your price sensor provides new data (15, 30, or 60 minutes).

prices.description: Configure your electricity price sensors for import and export pricing.
prices.title: Electricity Prices

data.hsem_ev_charger_force_max_discharge_powe: EV Charger Force Max Discharge Power
data.hsem_house_power_includes_ev_charger_pow: House Power Includes EV Charger Power

data_description.hsem_ev_allow_charge_past_target_soc: Enable this option if you want to allow the system to continue charging the EV battery even after it has reached the target state of charge (SoC). This can be useful in scenarios where you want to ensure the EV is fully charged regardless of the target setting.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_force_max_discharge_powe: Enable this if you want to force a specific maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_max_discharge_power: Set the maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_power: Select the sensor that measures the power consumption of your EV charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_status: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV charger is active (e.g., a switch or sensor).
data_description.hsem_ev_connected: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV is connected to the charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_enabled: Enable or disable the configuration for a second EV charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_soc: Select the sensor that provides the current state of charge (SoC) of your EV battery.
data_description.hsem_house_power_includes_ev_charger_pow: Enable this if the house consumption power sensor already includes the EV charger power consumption.

ev.description: Configure EV Charger settings for HSEM.
ev.title: EV Charger Settings (Optional)

data.hsem_ev_second_allow_charge_past_target_: Allow Charging Past Target SoC For Second EV (Advanced)
data.hsem_ev_second_charger_force_max_dischar: Second EV Charger Force Max Discharge Power
data.hsem_ev_second_charger_max_discharge_pow: Second EV Charger Max Discharge Power
data.hsem_ev_second_soc: Second EV State of Charge (SoC) Sensor

data_description.hsem_ev_second_allow_charge_past_target_: Enable this option if you want to allow the system to continue charging the EV battery even after it has reached the target state of charge (SoC). This can be useful in scenarios where you want to ensure the EV is fully charged regardless of the target setting.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_force_max_dischar: Enable this if you want to force a specific maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_max_discharge_pow: Set the maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_power: Select the sensor that measures the power consumption of your EV charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_status: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV charger is active (e.g., a switch or sensor).
data_description.hsem_ev_second_connected: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV is connected to the charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_soc: Select the sensor that provides the current state of charge (SoC) of your EV battery.

ev_second.description: Configure Second EV Charger settings for HSEM.
ev_second.title: Second EV Charger Settings (Optional)

data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_excess_pv_en: Huawei Batteries Excess PV energy use in TOU
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_end_of_disch: Huawei Batteries End of Discharge SOC (%)
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_charging_cut: Huawei Batteries Charging Cutoff Capacity (Max SoC %)
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_grid_charge_: Huawei Batteries Grid Charge Cutoff SOC (%)
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_state_of_cap: Huawei Batteries State of Capacity Sensor
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_tou_charging: Huawei Batteries TOU Charging and Discharging Periods
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_working_mode: Huawei Solar Batteries Working Mode Sensor
data.hsem_huawei_solar_inverter_active_power_: Huawei Inverter Active Power Control Sensor

data_description.hsem_batteries_charge_efficiency: Specify the charge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy stored in the battery equals the input energy multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.
data_description.hsem_batteries_discharge_efficiency: Specify the discharge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy delivered to the house equals the battery energy removed multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.
data_description.hsem_batteries_cycle_cost: Optional extra per-kWh cost of cycling the battery (EUR/kWh). Added on top of the auto-calculated depreciation threshold. Set to 0 to rely solely on the depreciation guard. Example: 0.02 means the planner requires 2 cent extra spread per kWh cycled before approving grid charging.
data_description.hsem_batteries_expected_cycles: Enter the expected number of full charge/discharge cycles over the battery's lifetime. Used to calculate depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold. Typical LiFePO4 batteries support 4000â€“8000 cycles.
data_description.hsem_batteries_purchase_price: Enter the total purchase price of your battery system in EUR. Used together with expected cycles and usable capacity to calculate the depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_excess_pv_en: Select the sensor that provides setting for excess PV energy use in TOU periods.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_forcible_cha: Sensor showing current forcible charge/discharge state (e.g. Stopped, Discharging at 5000W for 60 minutes)
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_end_of_disch: Specify the end of discharge SOC percentage for your Huawei batteries. When the battery reaches this SOC level, it will stop discharging to prevent deep discharge and potential damage to the battery.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_charging_cut: Select the entity that defines the maximum state of charge (SoC) the battery is allowed to reach during charging (register STORAGE_CHARGING_CUTOFF_CAPACITY). Typically 90â€“10â€¯%. Used by the SoC simulation as the upper SoC ceiling so the planner never schedules charging beyond this limit.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_grid_charge_: Specify the grid charge cutoff SOC percentage for your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_maximum_char: Select the sensor that provides the maximum charging power for your Huawei batteries in kilowatts (W).
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_maximum_disc: Select the sensor that provides the maximum discharging power for your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_rated_capaci: Select the sensor that provides the total rated capacity of your Huawei batteries in watts (W).
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_state_of_cap: Select the sensor that provides the state of charge (SOC) of your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_tou_charging: Select the sensor that defines the Time-of-Use (TOU) charging and discharging periods for your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_working_mode: Select the sensor that indicates the current working mode of your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_device_id_inverter_1: Select your primary Huawei inverter device.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_device_id_inverter_2: Select your secondary Huawei inverter device, if applicable.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_inverter_active_power_: Select the sensor that controls the active power output of your Huawei inverter.

huawei_solar.description: Configure the working mode for Huawei solar batteries.
huawei_solar.title: Huawei Solar Sensors

data_description.hsem_batteries_purchase_price: Enter the total purchase price of your battery system in EUR. Used together with expected cycles and usable capacity to calculate the depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold.
data_description.hsem_batteries_expected_cycles: Enter the expected number of full charge/discharge cycles over the battery's lifetime. Used to calculate depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold. Typical LiFePO4 batteries support 4000â€“8000 cycles.
data_description.hsem_batteries_cycle_cost: Optional extra per-kWh cost of cycling the battery (EUR/kWh). Added on top of the auto-calculated depreciation threshold. Set to 0 to rely solely on the depreciation guard. Example: 0.02 means the planner requires 2 cent extra spread per kWh cycled before approving grid charging.
data_description.hsem_batteries_capacity_loss_pct: Expected capacity loss at end-of-life as a percentage. LiFePO4 typically reaches 80% capacity after ~6000 cycles (20% loss). Default 30% adds margin for calendar ageing.
data_description.hsem_batteries_charge_efficiency: Specify the charge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy stored in the battery equals the input energy multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.
data_description.hsem_batteries_discharge_efficiency: Specify the discharge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy delivered to the house equals the battery energy removed multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.

battery_economics.description: Configure battery economics parameters that affect depreciation calculations and round-trip efficiency.
battery_economics.title: Battery Economics

data_description.device_name: Enter a unique name for this device.
data_description.hsem_extended_attributes: Enable this option to expose extra diagnostic attributes on the sensor entity.
data_description.hsem_planner_min_resolve_interval_minute: Minimum minutes between full plan re-solves. The optimizer keeps the existing plan and only smooths the current EV charger power between re-solves, preventing rapid on/off oscillation. Set to 0 to re-solve every cycle.
data_description.hsem_read_only: Enable this option to set the integration to read-only mode, preventing it from sending commands to devices.
data_description.hsem_recommendation_interval_length: The length in minutes of each recommendation interval used to calculate optimal battery usage.
data_description.hsem_recommendation_interval_minutes: The number of recommendation intervals to consider when calculating optimal battery usage.
data_description.hsem_update_interval: The update interval in minutes for the working mode sensor.
data_description.hsem_verbose_logging: Enable verbose debug logging for troubleshooting purposes.

data_description.hsem_batteries_enable_batteries_schedule: Enable or disable the first battery discharge schedule.
Specify the end time for the first battery discharge schedule.
Specify the start time for the first battery discharge schedule.
Enable or disable the second battery discharge schedule.
Specify the end time for the second battery discharge schedule.
Specify the start time for the second battery discharge schedule.
Enable or disable the third battery discharge schedule.
Specify the end time for the third battery discharge schedule.
Specify the start time for the third battery discharge schedule.

batteries_schedules.description: Configure up to three battery discharge schedule windows, each with enable/disable toggle, start time, and end time. Schedules define when the battery may discharge to cover peak consumption or export to the grid.
batteries_schedules.title: Battery Discharge Schedules

data_description.hsem_months_winter: Select the months that should be considered as Winter/Spring for the seasonal working mode adjustments. You can select multiple months.

months.description: Configure the months that are considered as Winter/Spring. During these months, the system will prioritize charging the batteries from grid energy to ensure sufficient battery levels during periods of lower solar production. Remaining months will be considered as Summer/Autum months.
months.title: Winter/Spring Months

data_description.hsem_house_consumption_power: Select the sensor that measures your house's total power consumption.
data_description.hsem_main_fuse_amps: Your house's main fuse/breaker rating in amps (25, 35, etc.).  The MILP optimizer will respect this limit when scheduling battery and EV charging.  Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.hsem_solar_production_power: Select the sensor that measures the power generated by your solar panels.

data_description.battery_soc: Auto-detected battery state of capacity sensor.
data_description.eod_soc: Auto-detected end of discharge SoC entity.
data_description.active_power_control: Auto-detected inverter active power control sensor.
data_description.solcast_today: Auto-detected Solcast PV forecast for today.
data_description.solcast_tomorrow: Auto-detected Solcast PV forecast for tomorrow.
data_description.use_quick_setup: Enable to use auto-detected entities and skip the full entity-by-entity setup wizard. Disable for Advanced Setup.

quick_setup.description: HSEM has auto-detected the following entities. {warning}Review the detected entities below. You can confirm to use them or choose Advanced Setup to select entities manually.
quick_setup.title: Quick Setup — Auto-Detected Entities

data.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_likeli: Solcast PV Forecast Preferred forecast likelihood to use

data_description.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_likeli: Select the preferred forecast likelihood to use for Solcast PV forecasts.
data_description.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_today: Select the sensor that provides today's PV production forecast from Solcast.
data_description.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_tomorr: Select the sensor that provides tomorrow's PV production forecast from Solcast.

data_description.device_name: Enter a unique name for this device.
data_description.hsem_extended_attributes: Enable this option to expose extra diagnostic attributes on the sensor entity.
data_description.hsem_planner_min_resolve_interval_minute: Minimum minutes between full plan re-solves. The optimizer keeps the existing plan and only smooths the current EV charger power between re-solves, preventing rapid on/off oscillation. Set to 0 to re-solve every cycle.
data_description.hsem_read_only: Enable this option to set the integration to read-only mode, preventing it from sending commands to devices.
data_description.hsem_recommendation_interval_length: The length in minutes of each recommendation interval used to calculate optimal battery usage.
data_description.hsem_recommendation_interval_minutes: The number of recommendation intervals to consider when calculating optimal battery usage.
data_description.hsem_update_interval: The update interval in minutes for the working mode sensor.
data_description.hsem_verbose_logging: Enable verbose debug logging for troubleshooting purposes.

data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_14d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 14 days.
data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_1d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 1 day.
data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_3d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 3 days.
data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_7d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 7 days.

weighted_values.description: Configure the weighted values for house consumption energy to estimate your average power usage. The system calculates the average consumption for each hour over 1, 3, 7, and 14 days. These averages are then weighted to provide a combined, weighted estimate. This approach helps to smooth out anomalies, such as unusually high consumption on a single day due to low prices, and provides a reliable prediction of your expected power usage in specific time intervals (e.g., between 16:00 and 17:00).
weighted_values.title: Weighted Values

data.hsem_ml_consumption_energy_entity: House consumption energy sensor (excl. battery & EV)

data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_enabled: When enabled, uses ridge regression on recorder history instead of rolling averages. Requires 14+ days of history.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_energy_entity: Cumulative house consumption energy meter (kWh). Must exclude battery AND EV charging - use a sensor upstream of both. Falls back to grid import if not set.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_history_days: Days of recorder history for ML training (7â€“90).
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_net_consumption: Subtract grid export from import for net house consumption.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_temperature_entity: Optionalâ€”outdoor temperature sensor in Â°C. Use an outdoor sensor, not an indoor thermostat.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_sequential: Feed each slot prediction as input to the next. Captures intra-hour momentum (e.g. cooking spikes carry forward).

energy_and_ml.description: Configure energy meters and enable ML-based consumption prediction.
energy_and_ml.title: Energy & ML

data_description.hsem_export_electricity_min_price: Set the minimum export price. Exports below this price will be blocked.
data_description.hsem_electricity_price_update_interval: How often your price sensor provides new data (15, 30, or 60 minutes).

prices.description: Configure your electricity price sensors for import and export pricing.
prices.title: Electricity Prices

data.hsem_ev_charger_force_max_discharge_powe: EV Charger Force Max Discharge Power
data.hsem_house_power_includes_ev_charger_pow: House Power Includes EV Charger Power

data_description.hsem_ev_allow_charge_past_target_soc: Enable this option if you want to allow the system to continue charging the EV battery even after it has reached the target state of charge (SoC). This can be useful in scenarios where you want to ensure the EV is fully charged regardless of the target setting.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_force_max_discharge_powe: Enable this if you want to force a specific maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_max_discharge_power: Set the maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_power: Select the sensor that measures the power consumption of your EV charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_charger_status: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV charger is active (e.g., a switch or sensor).
data_description.hsem_ev_connected: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV is connected to the charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_enabled: Enable or disable the configuration for a second EV charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_soc: Select the sensor that provides the current state of charge (SoC) of your EV battery.
data_description.hsem_house_power_includes_ev_charger_pow: Enable this if the house consumption power sensor already includes the EV charger power consumption.

ev.description: Configure EV Charger settings for HSEM.
ev.title: EV Charger Settings (Optional)

data.hsem_ev_second_allow_charge_past_target_: Allow Charging Past Target SoC For Second EV (Advanced)
data.hsem_ev_second_charger_force_max_dischar: Second EV Charger Force Max Discharge Power
data.hsem_ev_second_charger_max_discharge_pow: Second EV Charger Max Discharge Power
data.hsem_ev_second_soc: Second EV State of Charge (SoC) Sensor

data_description.hsem_ev_second_allow_charge_past_target_: Enable this option if you want to allow the system to continue charging the EV battery even after it has reached the target state of charge (SoC). This can be useful in scenarios where you want to ensure the EV is fully charged regardless of the target setting.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_force_max_dischar: Enable this if you want to force a specific maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_max_discharge_pow: Set the maximum discharge power for the Huawei batteries while EV is charging.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_power: Select the sensor that measures the power consumption of your EV charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_charger_status: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV charger is active (e.g., a switch or sensor).
data_description.hsem_ev_second_connected: Select the sensor or entity that indicates whether the EV is connected to the charger.
data_description.hsem_ev_second_soc: Select the sensor that provides the current state of charge (SoC) of your EV battery.

ev_second.description: Configure Second EV Charger settings for HSEM.
ev_second.title: Second EV Charger Settings (Optional)

data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_excess_pv_en: Huawei Batteries Excess PV energy use in TOU
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_end_of_disch: Huawei Batteries End of Discharge SOC (%)
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_charging_cut: Huawei Batteries Charging Cutoff Capacity (Max SoC %)
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_grid_charge_: Huawei Batteries Grid Charge Cutoff SOC (%)
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_state_of_cap: Huawei Batteries State of Capacity Sensor
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_tou_charging: Huawei Batteries TOU Charging and Discharging Periods
data.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_working_mode: Huawei Solar Batteries Working Mode Sensor
data.hsem_huawei_solar_inverter_active_power_: Huawei Inverter Active Power Control Sensor

data_description.hsem_batteries_charge_efficiency: Specify the charge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy stored in the battery equals the input energy multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.
data_description.hsem_batteries_discharge_efficiency: Specify the discharge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy delivered to the house equals the battery energy removed multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.
data_description.hsem_batteries_cycle_cost: Optional extra per-kWh cost of cycling the battery (EUR/kWh). Added on top of the auto-calculated depreciation threshold. Set to 0 to rely solely on the depreciation guard. Example: 0.02 means the planner requires 2 cent extra spread per kWh cycled before approving grid charging.
data_description.hsem_batteries_expected_cycles: Enter the expected number of full charge/discharge cycles over the battery's lifetime. Used to calculate depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold. Typical LiFePO4 batteries support 4000â€“8000 cycles.
data_description.hsem_batteries_purchase_price: Enter the total purchase price of your battery system in EUR. Used together with expected cycles and usable capacity to calculate the depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_excess_pv_en: Select the sensor that provides setting for excess PV energy use in TOU periods.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_forcible_cha: Sensor showing current forcible charge/discharge state (e.g. Stopped, Discharging at 5000W for 60 minutes)
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_end_of_disch: Specify the end of discharge SOC percentage for your Huawei batteries. When the battery reaches this SOC level, it will stop discharging to prevent deep discharge and potential damage to the battery.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_charging_cut: Select the entity that defines the maximum state of charge (SoC) the battery is allowed to reach during charging (register STORAGE_CHARGING_CUTOFF_CAPACITY). Typically 90â€“10â€¯%. Used by the SoC simulation as the upper SoC ceiling so the planner never schedules charging beyond this limit.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_grid_charge_: Specify the grid charge cutoff SOC percentage for your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_maximum_char: Select the sensor that provides the maximum charging power for your Huawei batteries in kilowatts (W).
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_maximum_disc: Select the sensor that provides the maximum discharging power for your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_rated_capaci: Select the sensor that provides the total rated capacity of your Huawei batteries in watts (W).
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_state_of_cap: Select the sensor that provides the state of charge (SOC) of your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_tou_charging: Select the sensor that defines the Time-of-Use (TOU) charging and discharging periods for your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_batteries_working_mode: Select the sensor that indicates the current working mode of your Huawei batteries.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_device_id_inverter_1: Select your primary Huawei inverter device.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_device_id_inverter_2: Select your secondary Huawei inverter device, if applicable.
data_description.hsem_huawei_solar_inverter_active_power_: Select the sensor that controls the active power output of your Huawei inverter.

huawei_solar.description: Configure the working mode for Huawei solar batteries.
huawei_solar.title: Huawei Solar Sensors

data_description.hsem_batteries_purchase_price: Enter the total purchase price of your battery system in EUR. Used together with expected cycles and usable capacity to calculate the depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold.
data_description.hsem_batteries_expected_cycles: Enter the expected number of full charge/discharge cycles over the battery's lifetime. Used to calculate depreciation cost per kWh and the recommended minimum export price threshold. Typical LiFePO4 batteries support 4000â€“8000 cycles.
data_description.hsem_batteries_cycle_cost: Optional extra per-kWh cost of cycling the battery (EUR/kWh). Added on top of the auto-calculated depreciation threshold. Set to 0 to rely solely on the depreciation guard. Example: 0.02 means the planner requires 2 cent extra spread per kWh cycled before approving grid charging.
data_description.hsem_batteries_capacity_loss_pct: Expected capacity loss at end-of-life as a percentage. LiFePO4 typically reaches 80% capacity after ~6000 cycles (20% loss). Default 30% adds margin for calendar ageing.
data_description.hsem_batteries_charge_efficiency: Specify the charge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy stored in the battery equals the input energy multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.
data_description.hsem_batteries_discharge_efficiency: Specify the discharge-side efficiency of your battery as a percentage (0â€“100). Energy delivered to the house equals the battery energy removed multiplied by this factor. Typical lithium batteries achieve 95â€“98 %. Defaults to 98 %.

battery_economics.description: Configure battery economics parameters that affect depreciation calculations and round-trip efficiency.
battery_economics.title: Battery Economics

data_description.device_name: Enter a unique name for this device.
data_description.hsem_extended_attributes: Enable this option to expose extra diagnostic attributes on the sensor entity.
data_description.hsem_planner_min_resolve_interval_minute: Minimum minutes between full plan re-solves. The optimizer keeps the existing plan and only smooths the current EV charger power between re-solves, preventing rapid on/off oscillation. Set to 0 to re-solve every cycle.
data_description.hsem_read_only: Enable this option to set the integration to read-only mode, preventing it from sending commands to devices.
data_description.hsem_recommendation_interval_length: The length in minutes of each recommendation interval used to calculate optimal battery usage.
data_description.hsem_recommendation_interval_minutes: The number of recommendation intervals to consider when calculating optimal battery usage.
data_description.hsem_update_interval: The update interval in minutes for the working mode sensor.
data_description.hsem_verbose_logging: Enable verbose debug logging for troubleshooting purposes.

data_description.hsem_batteries_enable_batteries_schedule: Enable or disable the first battery discharge schedule.
Specify the end time for the first battery discharge schedule.
Specify the start time for the first battery discharge schedule.
Enable or disable the second battery discharge schedule.
Specify the end time for the second battery discharge schedule.
Specify the start time for the second battery discharge schedule.
Enable or disable the third battery discharge schedule.
Specify the end time for the third battery discharge schedule.
Specify the start time for the third battery discharge schedule.

batteries_schedules.description: Configure up to three battery discharge schedule windows, each with enable/disable toggle, start time, and end time. Schedules define when the battery may discharge to cover peak consumption or export to the grid.
batteries_schedules.title: Battery Discharge Schedules

data_description.hsem_months_winter: Select the months that should be considered as Winter/Spring for the seasonal working mode adjustments. You can select multiple months.

months.description: Configure the months that are considered as Winter/Spring. During these months, the system will prioritize charging the batteries from grid energy to ensure sufficient battery levels during periods of lower solar production. Remaining months will be considered as Summer/Autum months.
months.title: Winter/Spring Months

data_description.hsem_house_consumption_power: Select the sensor that measures your house's total power consumption.
data_description.hsem_main_fuse_amps: Your house's main fuse/breaker rating in amps (25, 35, etc.).  The MILP optimizer will respect this limit when scheduling battery and EV charging.  Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.hsem_solar_production_power: Select the sensor that measures the power generated by your solar panels.

data.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_likeli: Solcast PV Forecast Preferred forecast likelihood to use

data_description.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_likeli: Select the preferred forecast likelihood to use for Solcast PV forecasts.
data_description.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_today: Select the sensor that provides today's PV production forecast from Solcast.
data_description.hsem_solcast_pv_forecast_forecast_tomorr: Select the sensor that provides tomorrow's PV production forecast from Solcast.

data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_14d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 14 days.
data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_1d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 1 day.
data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_3d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 3 days.
data_description.hsem_house_consumption_energy_weight_7d: Specify the weight percentage for the house consumption energy over the last 7 days.

weighted_values.description: Configure the weighted values for house consumption energy to estimate your average power usage. The system calculates the average consumption for each hour over 1, 3, 7, and 14 days. These averages are then weighted to provide a combined, weighted estimate. This approach helps to smooth out anomalies, such as unusually high consumption on a single day due to low prices, and provides a reliable prediction of your expected power usage in specific time intervals (e.g., between 16:00 and 17:00).
weighted_values.title: Weighted Values

data.hsem_ml_consumption_energy_entity: House consumption energy sensor (excl. battery & EV)

data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_enabled: When enabled, uses ridge regression on recorder history instead of rolling averages. Requires 14+ days of history.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_energy_entity: Cumulative house consumption energy meter (kWh). Must exclude battery AND EV charging - use a sensor upstream of both. Falls back to grid import if not set.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_history_days: Days of recorder history for ML training (7â€“90).
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_net_consumption: Subtract grid export from import for net house consumption.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_temperature_entity: Optionalâ€”outdoor temperature sensor in Â°C. Use an outdoor sensor, not an indoor thermostat.
data_description.hsem_ml_consumption_sequential: Feed each slot prediction as input to the next. Captures intra-hour momentum (e.g. cooking spikes carry forward).

energy_and_ml.description: Configure energy meters and enable ML-based consumption prediction.
energy_and_ml.title: Energy & ML

force_recalculation.name: Force recalculation
force_recalculation.description: Force the HSEM coordinator to run a full recalculation cycle immediately.

set_temporary_override.name: Set temporary override
set_temporary_override.description: Temporarily override the automatic planner by forcing a specific working mode.

working_mode.name: Working mode
working_mode.description: The working mode to force. Must be one of the supported override modes.

clear_override.name: Clear override
clear_override.description: Clear any active temporary working-mode override and return to automatic planner control.

export_diagnostics.name: Export diagnostics
export_diagnostics.description: Export a structured diagnostics dump for the HSEM integration with all entity IDs redacted.
